subject: OOM direct portal access
commit_message:
OOM direct portal access

-must include suffix of "onap.org" in URL for single signon
 -this isn't truly configurable - portal-app needs to be rebuilt
 See: portal/docs/tutorials/portal-sdk/single-signon.rst
-must update local etc hosts with "fake" dns names
-must use kubectl to portforward application ports into the pods
-fixed zookeeper service name,
  -this is needed to be able to login (auth)
-adding helper script to launch port-forwarding

Issue-ID: OOM-633

Change-Id: I019f33f71a673467c420d1e44ee6c42da4a95f4b
Signed-off-by: Mandeep Khinda <mandeep.khinda@amdocs.com>

entries.message:
Patch Set 4: Code-Review-1

(2 comments)

if we change api.simpledemo we need to change everywhere including BIND zone files in HEAT deployment and matching project files

Patch Set 4: Code-Review-1

I do not see the PODs coming up - 

Unable to mount volumes for pod "onap-portal-cassandra-8547d64978-vxhdn_onap(0b637fb2-497b-11e8-9ed1-0800272eb87c)": timeout expired waiting for volumes to attach/mount for pod "onap"/"onap-portal-cassandra-8547d64978-vxhdn". list of unattached/unmounted volumes=[onap-portal-cassandra-data]

What I did to test:

1. make all 
2. sudo helm install ./onap -n onap --namespace onap -f onap/resources/environments/disable-allcharts.yaml --set portal.enabled=true

Waited for 8 minutes - nothing changes.

Patch Set 4:

(2 comments)

Ikram,
did you already have an existing portal chart deployed?
do you have a k8s cluster or are you using a single node?
this patch doesn't affect pv/pvcs for the portal so your error is likely not related to this.

please purge any existing releases, delete the onap namespace, remove all data from /dockerdata-nfs and re-deploy the way you indicated in your comment.

Patch Set 5: Code-Review+1

(1 comment)

Adding Marco so we can coordinate update on the bind file and documentation/demo/training

(1 comment)

Hi Mandeep, posted comment on the launch-onap-portal.sh file. Policy seems to be missing from the list.

Patch Set 5:

(2 comments)

Thanks for the review guys!
I was able to bring up Policy and the xDemoApp with the changes to launch script.  I also realized that I hardcoded the namespace and was missing the helm release name so I added 2 parameters.
They fail the first time you click to launch the app, but if you close them and re-launch they load just fine.
